title: AI Provenance Tracking — Source Document, Page, Model, and Prompt Version Recorded
description: Every AI-generated requirement shall include provenance metadata: source document name, page number, section heading, Gemini model version, prompt template version, and extraction timestamp. Provenance is immutable and preserved through the requirement's lifecycle.

provenanceSchema.sourceTextSnippet: Full-time employees are eligible after 30 calendar days...

title: Confidence Score Required on All AI-Generated Requirements
description: Every AI-generated requirement shall include a confidence score (0.0–1.0) reflecting the AI's certainty about the extraction. The score shall drive the review priority: ≥0.90 pre-selected for approval, 0.70–0.89 requires human review, <0.70 requires manual examination.

confidenceBands.label: Medium Confidence
confidenceBands.reviewAction: Requires explicit human review before approval

confidenceBands.label: Low Confidence
confidenceBands.reviewAction: Manual examination required; AI may have misinterpreted context

title: AI-Generated Requirements Cannot Auto-Commit to Configuration Without Human Approval
description: No AI-generated requirement, conflict resolution, or plan recommendation shall be automatically applied to any production configuration. Every AI output must pass through a human review and explicit approval step before it can be used to create rules, modify plans, or update any configuration.
architecturalPrinciple: AI accelerates human experts; it does not replace them. The approval chain is: AI Generated → Human Reviewed → Explicitly Approved → Configuration Applied.
prohibitedBehaviors: Auto-approve based on confidence threshold alone
Silent background application of AI recommendations

title: AI Hallucination Control — Source Citation Must Link to Verifiable Document Page
description: AI-generated requirements shall include a source citation that links to a specific document, page number, and highlighted text. If the AI cannot identify a source citation with high confidence, it shall flag the requirement with UNVERIFIED_SOURCE status rather than fabricating a citation.
uiDisplay: Requirements with CITATION_UNVERIFIED displayed with ⚠ Source Unverified indicator; requires mandatory human review

title: Visual Status Badges — AI Generated vs Human Reviewed vs Approved on All Requirements
description: Every requirement in the Requirements Studio shall display a visual status badge indicating its governance state: [AI] AI Generated, [HR] Human Reviewed, [✓] Approved, [✗] Rejected, [↩] Needs Clarification. These badges appear in all list views, detail panels, and traceability maps.

governanceStates.description: Raw AI output, not yet reviewed
Reviewed by human, pending formal approval
Rejected; archived but visible for audit
Referred back to AI Interview for clarification

title: Human Approval Required Before AI Requirement Maps to Plan or Rule Configuration
description: The [Create Rule] and [Apply to Plan] actions shall be enabled only for requirements in the APPROVED state. Attempting to map an AI_GENERATED or HUMAN_REVIEWED requirement to configuration shall display an advisory: 'Approve this requirement before applying to plan configuration.'

title: AI Model Version and Prompt Template Version Recorded with Each Extraction Run
description: Each AI document extraction run shall record: Gemini model version, prompt template ID and version, extraction timestamp, document SHA-256 fingerprint, and the raw AI response (stored separately from the parsed requirement). This enables reproducibility and prompt iteration tracking.

title: AI Explainability — 'Why Did AI Generate This?' Audit Trail on Each Requirement
description: The Requirements Studio detail panel shall display an AI Reasoning section for each AI-generated requirement, showing: the source text snippet, the AI's extraction rationale (summary of why it was identified as a requirement), and the confidence score explanation.

title: AI Conflict Classification: CONTRADICTION / OVERLAP / AMBIGUITY / MISSING / DUPLICATE
description: AI conflict detection shall classify each detected issue into one of five categories. Each category has a distinct severity default and resolution workflow.

conflictTypes.description: Two documents state directly opposing rules
conflictTypes.example: Doc A: 30-day wait. Doc B: 60-day wait.

conflictTypes.description: Two rules cover the same population differently
conflictTypes.example: Doc A: FT employees eligible. Doc B: 30+ hr employees eligible.

conflictTypes.description: A requirement uses undefined or vague language
conflictTypes.example: Employees are eligible after completing the required waiting period. (No period defined.)

conflictTypes.description: A process is referenced but no supporting rule exists
conflictTypes.example: Plan requires dependent verification but no verification process is specified.

conflictTypes.description: Same requirement stated in two documents with minor wording variation
conflictTypes.example: Both Benefits Guide and Eligibility Policy state the 30-day rule in identical terms.

title: Bulk AI Actions Require Explicit Human Confirmation — No Silent Batch Apply
description: Any bulk AI action (Approve All, Reject All, Apply All to Plan) shall require an explicit confirmation dialog listing the count and summary of items affected. Auto-application of AI batch recommendations without confirmation is prohibited.
confirmationDialogContent: Action summary: N items affected | Preview of items | [Confirm] [Cancel]

title: AI Errors Must Be Human-Recoverable — No Silent AI Failures
description: When an AI operation fails, the system shall surface a clear, actionable error message to the user. The error shall identify the operation, what failed, and what the user can do (retry, run in background, view partial results). Silent AI failures — where an operation fails without user notification — are prohibited.

title: No AI-Generated Content Modifies Published Plan or Rule Without Explicit Approval Workflow
description: AI-generated content (requirements, conflict resolutions, impact analysis recommendations) shall only affect configuration in DRAFT state. Published plans and active eligibility rules cannot be modified by AI action. Changes always require the human-approved workflow: AI Output → Requirement Approval → Draft Change → Human Review → Publish.

title: AI Governance Dashboard — Pending Review Count and Extraction Health Summary
description: The AI Requirements section header shall display a governance summary: total extracted, pending review, approved, rejected, unverified citations, and active conflicts. This gives the Requirements Analyst an immediate quality picture on entry to the workspace.

title: Reviewer Assignment — AI Requirements Can Be Assigned to Specific Analyst
description: An AI-generated requirement can be assigned to a specific Requirements Analyst or Benefits Administrator for review. Assigned reviewer is notified (in-app notification) when the requirement is ready for review. Unassigned requirements are visible to all authorized reviewers.
assignmentModel: Optional; unassigned requirements are unowned and visible to all authorized roles
notificationTrigger: Assignment created → in-app notification to assigned reviewer

title: AI Provenance Chain: Document → Extraction Run → Requirement → Review → Approval → Configuration
description: The complete provenance chain for every approved requirement shall be navigable in the Audit & Traceability workspace. Starting from any approved requirement, a user shall be able to trace backwards to the source PDF page and forward to the configuration it affects.
provenanceChain: Source PDF page → Extraction Run → AI-Generated Requirement → Human Review event → Approval event → Configuration artifact (Rule / Plan)
